heading: Biomass Crop Assistance Program
label: 8111 Biomass Crop Assistance Program

children.heading: BCAP
children.label: 1 BCAP
children.text: The term “BCAP” means the Biomass Crop Assistance Program established under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: has specified boundaries that are submitted to the Secretary by the project sponsor and subsequently approved by the Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: includes producers with contract acreage that will supply a portion of the renewable biomass needed by a biomass conversion facility; and

children.label: C
children.text: is physically located within an economically practicable distance from the biomass conversion facility.

children.heading: Contract acreage
children.label: 3 Contract acreage
children.text: The term “contract acreage” means eligible land that is covered by a BCAP contract entered into with the Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “eligible crop” means a crop of renewable biomass.

children.label: i
children.text: any crop that is eligible to receive payments under title I of the Agricultural Act of 2014 [ et seq.] or an amendment made by that title; or7 U.S.C. 9001

children.label: ii
children.text: any plant that is invasive or noxious or species or varieties of plants that credible risk assessment tools or other credible sources determine are potentially invasive, as determined by the Secretary in consultation with other appropriate Federal or State departments and agencies.

children.label: i
children.text: agricultural and nonindustrial private forest lands (as defined in ); andsection 2103a(c) of title 16

children.label: ii
children.text: land enrolled in the conservation reserve program established under subchapter B of chapter I of subtitle D of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.), or the Agricultural Conservation Easement Program established under subtitle H of title XII of that Act [ et seq.], under a contract that will expire at the end of the current fiscal year.16 U.S.C. 383116 U.S.C. 3865

children.label: i
children.text: Federal- or State-owned land;

children.label: ii
children.text: land that is native sod, as of the date of enactment of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 ( et seq.);7 U.S.C. 8701

children.label: iii
children.text: land enrolled in the conservation reserve program established under subchapter B of chapter 1 of subtitle D of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.), other than land described in subparagraph (A)(ii); or16 U.S.C. 3831

children.label: iv
children.text: land enrolled in the Agricultural Conservation Easement Program established under subtitle H of title XII of that Act [ et seq.], other than land described in subparagraph (A)(ii).16 U.S.C. 3865

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “eligible material” means renewable biomass harvested directly from the land, including crop residue from any crop that is eligible to receive payments under title I of the Agricultural Act of 2014 [ et seq.] or an amendment made by that title.7 U.S.C. 9001

children.label: aa
children.text: National Forest System;

children.label: bb
children.text: Bureau of Land Management land;

children.label: cc
children.text: non-Federal land; or

children.label: dd
children.text: land owned by an individual Indian or Indian tribe that is held in trust by the United States for the benefit of the individual Indian or Indian tribe or subject to a restriction against alienation imposed by the United States;

children.label: aa
children.text: a conservation plan;

children.label: bb
children.text: a forest stewardship plan; or

children.label: cc
children.text: a plan that the Secretary determines is equivalent to a plan described in item (aa) or (bb) and consistent with Executive Order 13112 ( note; relating to invasive species);42 U.S.C. 4321

children.label: I
children.text: is a byproduct of a preventative treatment that is removed to reduce hazardous fuel or to reduce or contain disease or insect infestation; and

children.label: II
children.text: if harvested from Federal land, is harvested in accordance with ;section 6512(e) of title 16

children.label: iii
children.text: eligible material that is delivered to a qualified biomass conversion facility to be used for heat, power, biobased products, research, or advanced biofuels; and

children.label: iv
children.text: algae.

children.label: I
children.text: barley, corn, grain sorghum, oats, rice, or wheat;

children.label: II
children.text: honey;

children.label: III
children.text: mohair;

children.label: IV
children.text: oilseeds, including canola, crambe, flaxseed, mustard seed, rapeseed, safflower seed, soybeans, sesame seed, and sunflower seed;

children.label: V
children.text: peanuts;

children.label: VI
children.text: pulse;

children.label: VII
children.text: chickpeas, lentils, and dry peas;

children.label: VIII
children.text: dairy products;

children.label: IX
children.text: sugar; and

children.label: X
children.text: wool and cotton boll fiber;

children.label: ii
children.text: animal waste and byproducts, including fat, oil, grease, and manure;

children.label: iii
children.text: food waste and yard waste;

children.label: I
children.text: is removed outside contract acreage; and

children.label: II
children.text: is not a byproduct of a preventative treatment to reduce hazardous fuel or to reduce or contain disease or insect infestation;

children.label: v
children.text: any woody eligible material collected or harvested outside contract acreage that would otherwise be used for existing market products; or

children.label: vi
children.text: bagasse.

children.heading: Producer
children.label: 7 Producer
children.text: The term “producer” means an owner or operator of contract acreage that is physically located within a BCAP project area.

children.label: A
children.text: a group of producers; or

children.label: B
children.text: a biomass conversion facility.

children.heading: Socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher
children.label: 9 Socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher
children.text: The term “socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher” has the meaning given the term in section 2279(e) of this title.1See References in Text note below.1
support the establishment and production of eligible crops for conversion to bioenergy in selected BCAP project areas; and
assist agricultural and forest land owners and operators with the collection, harvest, storage, and transportation of eligible material for use in a biomass conversion facility.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall provide financial assistance to a producer of an eligible crop in a BCAP project area.

children.label: i
children.text: a description of the eligible land and eligible crops of each producer that will participate in the proposed BCAP project area;

children.label: ii
children.text: a letter of commitment from a biomass conversion facility that the facility will use the eligible crops intended to be produced in the proposed BCAP project area;

children.label: iii
children.text: evidence that the biomass conversion facility has sufficient equity available, as determined by the Secretary, if the biomass conversion facility is not operational at the time the proposal is submitted to the Secretary; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any other information about the biomass conversion facility or proposed biomass conversion facility that the Secretary determines necessary for the Secretary to be reasonably assured that the plant will be in operation by the date on which the eligible crops are ready for harvest.

children.heading: BCAP project area selection criteria
children.label: B BCAP project area selection criteria
i
children.text: the volume of the eligible crops proposed to be produced in the proposed BCAP project area and the probability that those crops will be used for the purposes of the BCAP;

children.label: ii
children.text: the volume of renewable biomass projected to be available from sources other than the eligible crops grown on contract acres;

children.label: iii
children.text: the anticipated economic impact in the proposed BCAP project area;

children.label: iv
children.text: the opportunity for producers and local investors to participate in the ownership of the biomass conversion facility in the proposed BCAP project area;

children.label: I
children.text: beginning farmers or ranchers (as defined in accordance with ); orsection 1991(a) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers;

children.label: vi
children.text: the impact on soil, water, and related resources;

children.label: I
children.text: agronomic conditions;

children.label: II
children.text: harvest and postharvest practices; and

children.label: III
children.text: monoculture and polyculture crop mixes;

children.label: viii
children.text: the range of eligible crops among project areas;

children.label: ix
children.text: existing project areas that have received funding under this section and the continuation of funding of such project areas to advance the maturity of such project areas; and

children.label: x
children.text: any additional information that the Secretary determines to be necessary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: On approval of a BCAP project area by the Secretary, each producer in the BCAP project area shall enter into a contract directly with the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: an agreement to make available to the Secretary, or to an institution of higher education or other entity designated by the Secretary, such information as the Secretary considers to be appropriate to promote the production of eligible crops and the development of biomass conversion technology;

children.label: ii
children.text: compliance with the highly erodible land conservation requirements of subtitle B of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.) and the wetland conservation requirements of subtitle C of title XII of that Act ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 381116 U.S.C. 3821

children.label: I
children.text: a conservation plan;

children.label: II
children.text: a forest stewardship plan; or

children.label: III
children.text: a plan that is equivalent to a conservation or forest stewardship plan; and

children.label: iv
children.text: any additional requirements that Secretary determines to be necessary.2So in original. Probably should be preceded by “the”.2

children.label: i
children.text: 5 years for annual and perennial crops; or

children.label: ii
children.text: 15 years for woody biomass.

children.heading: Relationship to other programs
children.label: 4 Relationship to other programs
children.text: In carrying out this subsection, the Secretary shall provide for the preservation of cropland base and yield history applicable to the land enrolled in a BCAP contract.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make establishment and annual payments directly to producers to support the establishment and production of eligible crops on contract acreage.

children.label: I
children.text: the cost of seeds and stock for perennials;

children.label: II
children.text: the cost of planting the perennial crop, as determined by the Secretary; and

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of nonindustrial private forestland, the costs of site preparation and tree planting.

children.heading: Socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers
children.label: ii Socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers
children.text: In the case of socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers, the costs of establishment may not exceed $750 per acre.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subject to clause (ii), the amount of an annual payment under this subsection shall be determined by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: an eligible crop is used for purposes other than the production of energy at the biomass conversion facility;

children.label: II
children.text: an eligible crop is delivered to the biomass conversion facility;

children.label: III
children.text: the producer receives a payment under subsection (d);

children.label: IV
children.text: the producer violates a term of the contract; or

children.label: V
children.text: the Secretary determines a reduction is necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: D Exclusion
children.text: The Secretary shall not make any BCAP payments on land for which payments are received under the conservation reserve program established under subchapter B of chapter 1 of subtitle D of title XII of the Food Security Act of 1985 ( et seq.) or the agricultural conservation easement program established under subtitle H of title XII of that Act [ et seq.].16 U.S.C. 383116 U.S.C. 3865

children.heading: Assistance with collection, harvest, storage, and transportation
children.label: d Assistance with collection, harvest, storage, and transportation
A
children.text: a producer of an eligible crop that is produced on BCAP contract acreage; or

children.label: B
children.text: a person with the right to collect or harvest eligible material, regardless of whether the eligible material is produced on contract acreage.

children.label: i
children.text: collection;

children.label: ii
children.text: harvest;

children.label: iii
children.text: storage; and

children.label: iv
children.text: transportation to a biomass conversion facility.

children.heading: Amount
children.label: B Amount
children.text: Subject to paragraph (3), the Secretary may provide matching payments at a rate of up to $1 for each $1 per ton provided by the biomass conversion facility, in an amount not to exceed $20 per dry ton for a period of 2 years.

children.heading: Limitation on assistance for BCAP contract acreage
children.label: 3 Limitation on assistance for BCAP contract acreage
children.text: As a condition of the receipt of an annual payment under subsection (c), a producer receiving a payment under this subsection for collection, harvest, storage, or transportation of an eligible crop produced on BCAP acreage shall agree to a reduction in the annual payment.

children.heading: Report
children.label: e Report
children.text: Not later than 4 years after , the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate a report on the dissemination by the Secretary of the best practice data and information gathered from participants receiving assistance under this section.February 7, 2014

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 1 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $25,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.

children.heading: Collection, harvest, storage, and transportation payments
children.label: 2 Collection, harvest, storage, and transportation payments
children.text: Of the amount made available under paragraph (1) for each fiscal year, the Secretary shall use not less than 10 percent, nor more than 50 percent, of the amount to make collection, harvest, transportation, and storage payments under subsection (d)(2).

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: 3 Technical assistance
children.text: Effective for fiscal year 2014 and each subsequent fiscal year, funds made available under this subsection shall be available for the provision of technical assistance with respect to activities authorized under this section.
